problems.title: Implement Queue using Stacks
problems.prompt:
Implement a first-in-first-out (FIFO) queue using only two stacks. The implemented queue should support all the functions of a normal queue: push, peek, pop, and empty.

Implement MyQueue with:
- push(x): Push element x to the back
- pop(): Remove and return the element from the front
- peek(): Return the element at the front
- empty(): Return true if empty
constraints: All calls to pop/peek are valid

problems.title: Number of Recent Calls
problems.prompt:
You have a RecentCounter class which counts the number of recent requests within a certain time frame.

Implement RecentCounter:
- RecentCounter() Initializes the counter with zero recent requests.
- int ping(int t) Adds a new request at time t (milliseconds), and returns the number of requests in the inclusive range [t - 3000, t].

It is guaranteed that every call to ping uses a strictly larger value of t than the previous call.
constraints: Each test case will call ping with strictly increasing t

problems.title: Design Circular Queue
problems.prompt:
Design your implementation of the circular queue. The circular queue is a linear data structure in which the operations are performed based on FIFO principle and the last position is connected back to the first to make a circle.

Implement MyCircularQueue:
- MyCircularQueue(k): Construct with size k
- enQueue(value): Insert at rear; return true if successful
- deQueue(): Delete from front; return true if successful
- Front(): Get front item; return -1 if empty
- Rear(): Get last item; return -1 if empty
- isEmpty() / isFull()

samples.input: k=3; enQueue 1,2,3,4; Rear; isFull; deQueue; enQueue 4; Rear

problems.title: Design Hit Counter
problems.prompt:
Design a hit counter which counts the number of hits received in the past 5 minutes (i.e., the past 300 seconds).

Implement HitCounter:
- HitCounter(): Initialize
- void hit(int timestamp): Record a hit at timestamp (in seconds)
- int getHits(int timestamp): Return the number of hits in the past 5 minutes from timestamp (i.e. in (timestamp - 300, timestamp])

Timestamps are in chronological order for both methods.
constraints: All calls are made with non-decreasing timestamps
At most 300 calls to hit and getHits

problems.title: Moving Average from Data Stream
problems.prompt:
Given a stream of integers and a window size, calculate the moving average of all integers in the sliding window.

Implement MovingAverage:
- MovingAverage(int size): Initialize with window size
- double next(int val): Returns the moving average of the last size values of the stream

problems.title: Time Needed to Buy Tickets
problems.prompt:
There are n people in a line buying tickets. The 0-th person wants tickets[0] tickets, and so on. Each person takes exactly 1 second to buy a ticket, then goes to the end of the line (if they still need more). If a person has no tickets left to buy, they leave.

Return the time it takes for the person at position k (0-indexed) to finish buying tickets.

problems.title: Sliding Window Maximum
problems.prompt:
You are given an array of integers nums, and an integer k representing a sliding window of size k moving from left to right. Return an array of the maximum values in each window.

Use a monotonic deque for an O(n) solution.

problems.title: Shortest Subarray with Sum at Least K
problems.prompt:
Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the length of the shortest non-empty subarray of nums with a sum of at least k. If there is no such subarray, return -1.

Hint: prefix sums + monotonic deque.
